text.title: BA Governance Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every decision type (new requirement / scope change / priority change / baseline update) names a single authority + escalation owner + artefact reference.
rule.rationale: Anonymous decision rights make every decision tribal knowledge; named authority + escalation lets the team route without drama.

rule.statement: Change-control workflow has 5 explicit steps: CR submission → impact assessment (T-shirt sized) → authority review → decision (Approve / Reject / Defer with owner+date) → baseline update.
rule.rationale: Open-ended CRs accumulate; explicit step gating prevents scope drift.

rule.statement: Communication plan names per audience: information / format / frequency / channel / feedback mechanism. Generic 'we'll keep them informed' rejected.
rule.rationale: Unspecified communication defaults to no communication; per-audience matrix enforces cadence.

rule.statement: Every governance artefact carries owner + last_reviewed + version + decision history. Undated, unowned artefacts rejected.
rule.rationale: Regulated builds require audit trail; undated artefacts fail audit.

rule.statement: Governance artefact re-validated every 30 days; older artefacts flagged stale.
rule.rationale: Stakeholder map drifts; 90-day-old map anchors to obsolete contacts and decision rights.

rule.statement: Skip for solo founder / single-team early MVP OR pure engineering refactor with no external stakeholders OR research spikes OR crisis incidents (use incident command).
rule.rationale: Methodology overhead exceeds value on small / low-stakes setups; incidents need incident-command, not governance workflow.
